title: viola stoloniflora
intro: Viola stoloniflora, also known as the yellow marsh violet or creeping violet, is a perennial plant species belonging to the violet family (Violaceae). The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and roots, which contain flavonoids and terpenoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Viola stoloniflora include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and provide antioxidant protection. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by drying the leaves and roots or by infusing them in hot water or oil.
